concept.domains: (topic concept) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c concept oceConcept-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
concept.title: Repair & Evaluate

abstract.p: In the Repair & Evaluate screen, you can see and enter data relevant to this service visit.

entry.p: Section
Description
eKOM/Technician ID

li.p: If it is not automatically retrieved from the SAM-key, you can enter or change it manually.
The retrieve button reads it from the SAM-key.
This is a mandatory field.

entry.p: Reasons/issues

li.p: All reasons and issues that you entered in the Collect screen are listed in red.
Click on each line to indicate in detail if and how you solved the issue.
This is mandatory selection. The check boxes with a solid line must be checked.

entry.p: One of the following pop-up window will appear:
Select the status at the end of the installation visit. The description field is optional.
Describe what you did in the window and indicate:

li.p: Done: you fixed this.
Postpone: postpone this action to the next visit.
Clear: clear all information from this reason / issue.

entry.p: When you select Done there will be a checkmark visible next to the Reason/issue.

Depending on the products group configuration, you will see either three actions (Done, Postpone, No action needed) as shown above, or a list with actions predefined for a product group, including Postpone and No action needed.
Depending on the product groups configuration, you may also see an additional area selection to indicate where the action has been executed.

note.p: Both the action selection and area selection are depending on the product. You can choose action selection without area selection. But you cannot choose area selection without action selection.

entry.p: When all selections are made, the results are shown in an updated issue list.
Errors and warnings

li.p: A list of all errors and warnings that occurred are listed in black. You judge yourself if they need attention.
Click each line to indicate if and how you solved the error/warning.
This is an optional field

entry.p: The following pop-up window will appear.
Describe what you did in the window and indicate:

li.p: Done: you fixed the error/warning.
Postpone: you investigated the issue but decided to postpone it to a next visit.
No action needed: you investigated the issue and concluded that no action is needed.
Clear: clear all information about this Reason/issue.

entry.p: Maintenance
The section shows all maintenance actions required for this service visit, individual maintenance items or grouped maintenance items.

li.p: Grouped maintenance items are marked bold. You can click the group to see the individual maintenance items.
Maintenance items in red and with checkboxes with a solid line are mandatory.
Maintenance items in black and with checkboxes with a dashed line are optional.

entry.p: There are 2 types of groups:

li.p: Asynchronous groups
Synchronous groups
Synchronous groups are shown with the symbol .

entry.p: Items within an asynchronous group have their own individual counter/time reset possibility. All items which are not marked Done will be displayed in the work list again at the next visit.

Items within a synchronous group have one common counter/time reset. This is linked to the header of the group. If the header is marked Done, the counter/time is reset for all items that belong to the group, even if an individual item was not completed. If this is the case, a yellow warning with an exclamation mark will be displayed.
Click each line to indicate if and how you executed the maintenance action. The following pop-up window appears:
Enter what you did in the window and indicate:

li.p: If the maintenance action requires parts, they can be selected in the list "Replaced parts".
Done & Reset: you performed this maintenance action.
Postpone: postpone this maintenance action to the next visit.
Clear: clear all information from this maintenance action.

entry.p: When you selected "Done & Reset" there will be a checkmark next to the maintenance action.
Other used parts
Parts which are not related to predefined maintenance actions have to be added by using the Add Part button.
The Add Part button shows the following pop-up window:

li.p: You can browse through the parts list or search by typing the part name or number.

When you find the part, click the part and then click Add Part to add the part to other used parts. Parts can appear at multiple locations. Please choose the part at the location where you replaced it.
When all parts have been added, use Close to return to the Repair & Evaluate screen.

entry.p: To delete a part, use the Delete button.
Remarks previous visit
Remarks that may be of use for a next service visit, are listed here, for example: an adjustment from this visit that needs a check during the next visit.
Recurring remark
Remarks that may be of use for all service visits, are listed here, for example: a remark about a frequent error/condition on this particular system.
